summary: Iteration 5 backend testing complete for the reservation zones + admin capacity feature. Wrote /app/backend/tests/test_reservations_zones_iter5.py (22 new tests across 7 test classes covering A-G from the review request). Executed pytest against the local backend (http://localhost:8001) covering both new and existing suites — 93/93 tests pass (22 new + 71 existing regression). Capacity is snapshotted/restored at module teardown so subsequent runs start clean. No backend defects discovered.
critical_code_review_comments: Capacity helper _get_capacity seeds defaults atomically via upsert — good.
_ensure_can_book validates zone whitelist and guests range (1..20) before doing DB work — good.
Admin PUT bounds-check 0..500 is enforced after auth/role check (proper ordering).
Existing reservations untouched when capacity is lowered (no retroactive cancellation) — confirmed by test E.test_lower_cap_below_existing_bookings_rejects_new.
seed_data_creation: Test creates ephemeral OTP-verified customers (TEST Zones Customer) and reservations on dates 2026-08-01..2026-08-05, 2026-09-09, 2026-09-15, 2026-09-20. Module-scoped autouse fixture pre-cleans those slots before the run and deletes them after, also restoring app_settings.capacity to its pre-test value (indoor=30, terrace=20 in this environment). No production/seed collections (menu, admin user) modified.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend reservation-zones + admin capacity feature is fully covered and green. New tests reuse /app/backend/tests pytest conventions (BASE_URL from EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL / EXPO_BACKEND_URL with /api prefix, admin login via email+password, customer via OTP). The iter5 file snapshots capacity at the start of the module and restores it on teardown — if you add new tests that change capacity, do the same. To run only iter5: `python -m pytest backend/tests/test_reservations_zones_iter5.py -v`. Full suite: `python -m pytest backend/tests/ -v`. Frontend was not touched per request.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no defects found. Previous iteration_5 dispatch had not actually executed tests; this run wrote the test file and executed pytest with JUnit output. All 22 new + 71 existing tests pass.
